domain: earth systems / fluvial geomorphology (river meander cutoff and oxbow lake formation)
specific_mechanism: A meandering river progressively widens its bends via differential erosion (outer bank) and deposition (inner bank); when bend curvature exceeds a critical threshold, the river breaches the narrow neck of land between adjacent bends and a 'cutoff' forms — the new straight channel takes over while the abandoned bend becomes an oxbow lake; cutoffs nonlocally accelerate upstream and downstream channel migration
llm_application: Treat a long agent context window as a meandering channel that progressively builds redundant loops (re-discussed topics, restated facts, repeated sub-goals); identify when the geometry of the discussion exceeds a 'critical sinuosity' and execute a 'cutoff' — the agent reroutes attention through a direct channel, leaving the redundant loop as an oxbow (preserved but disconnected from the main flow). The mechanism is automatic context pruning driven by geometric sinuosity threshold rather than fixed window or summarization.
novelty_claim: Existing context compression uses fixed-window truncation or summarization. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a fluvial-cutoff-style geometric-sinuosity-threshold-driven attention rerouting that leaves intact-but-disconnected oxbows of prior context.
